repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Photographs and negatives of Sarawak, other places in Malaysia, Indonesia, and elsewhere in Southeast Asia.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Hedda Morrison was born Hedda Hammer in Stuttgart, Germany, in 1908. As a young child she contracted polio, which caused her to walk with a limp. In 1929 she completed her secondary education at the Queen Katherine Convent (Königen-Katherina-Stift Gymnasium für Mädchen) in Stuttgart, and was sent to the University of Innsbruck in Austria to study medicine. After a few months she persuaded her parents to allow her to enroll in the State Institute for Photography in Munich (Bäyerische Staatslehranstalt für Lichtbildwesen in München).

After completing her studies, Hedda found paid work difficult to find as a result of the Depression. She decided to travel to China in 1933 in response to an advertisement in a photography journal. Between 1933 and 1938 she managed Hartung's, a German-owned commercial photographic studio in Peking. After her contract there expired in 1938, she continued to work as a freelance photographer. Hedda's photographs of Peking were well known, and she sold many prints and albums to wealthy overseas visitors as souvenirs.

In 1940 Hedda met Alastair Morrison and they married in Peking in 1946. Due to the increasing instability of the political situation in China, they left Peking soon after. The Morrison's spent six months in Hong Kong before relocating to Sarawak, in the north-west of the island of Borneo, where Alastair was appointed to the British Colonial Service and later became a district officer. Throughout her 20-year residence in Sarawak, Hedda accompanied Alastair on all his official journeys and made numerous independent photographic tours. From 1960-66 Hedda was employed by the Sarawak government to work part-time in the photographic section of the Information Office is Kuching. Her duties included taking photographs, establishing a photographic library and training government photographers. Hedda wrote two major books on Sarawak, Sarawak (1957) and Life in a Longhouse (1962).
In 1967 the Morrisons settled in Canberra, Australia. Hedda died in Canberra in 1991, at the age of 82.
Adapted from: "In her view: the photographs of Hedda Morrison in China and Sarawak 1933-67," exhibition catalogue, Powerhouse Museum, Sydney, Australia

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Hedda Morrison photographs, #4516. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Photographs and negatives of Sarawak, other places in Malaysia, Indonesia, and elsewhere in Southeast Asia.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: 2007 and 2008 China Heritage Calendars, and 2009, 2010, and 2015 Hong Kong Heritage Calendars, with photographs by Hedda Morrison, edited by Edward Stokes.

did.unittitle: Loose materials from Bakong, Tinjar, Tutoh, Patah notebook
Loose materials from Visit to 2nd division travel notebook
Tour to Thailand, Vietnam, Laos, and Burma
Loose materials from Brunei, Sarawak, Malaysia, and Singapore notebook

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Many contact sheets and photographs have original notes, inscriptions, or the photographer's stamp verso.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Titles and dates come from Morrison's original box labels except for Sarawak photographs which were undated on box labels.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives for these prints appear in Morrison negative box 22, labeled Borneo Evangelical Mission 1964.

did.unittitle: Mount Hagen, Goroka, Papua New Guinea
Mount Hagen, Goroka, Papua New Guinea

did.physdesc: Contact sheets, some with handwritten notes about subjects on verso.

did.unittitle: Material from trip to Laos and Vietnam

did.physdesc: Contact sheets, some separated from full sheet.
Color slides, most in mounts, two unmounted

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original label notes that these were returned from the Australian National Gallery.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original label notes that these were returned from the Australian National Gallery.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Some of the photographs in this set appear in Morrison's publication Sarawak, in the chapter on Penan. These are some of the few finished prints in the collection.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes images of the coast and villages.

did.unittitle: Prints for the Monash publication in development in Sarawak
Loose prints from Madagascar and Mauritius Kodak albums
The Penan People of Borneo album
Men of the Sea: Tropical Shorelife in Sarawak notebook and photographs
did.physdesc: Journal with photographs adhered on each page, text below.
Journal with photographs adhered on each page, text below.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Negatives are in the original order given them by the Morrisons, arranged by location and subject.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Titles come from Morrison's original box labels.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to negatives restricted to the permission of the curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: No prints exist in the collection for these negatives.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Morrison negatives box 22. Morrison notes categories for Sarawak (published 1957), Life in a Longhouse (published 1962), Melagu, Penan set (unpublished), Malay coastal set (unpublished), an unpublished Donald Moore set, and useful additional negatives.

did.unittitle: Malaya, Malaysia, Sabah, Brunei, Sarawak, Singapore
Sarawak, People and events around Kuching

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Title derived from dividers inside box.

did.unittitle: Sarawak (selected special), Solomon Islands, Burma, Vietnam, Canberra

processinfo.p: Title derived from dividers inside box.

did.unittitle: Newspaper clippings and photograph of Sarawak
